heading: Civil penalties
label: 1833a Civil penalties
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Financial Institutions Reform, Recovery, and Enforcement Act of 1989, and not as part of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Whoever violates any provision of law to which this section is made applicable by subsection (c) shall be subject to a civil penalty in an amount assessed by the court in a civil action under this section.

children.heading: Generally
children.label: 1 Generally
children.text: The amount of the civil penalty shall not exceed $1,000,000.

children.heading: Special rule for continuing violations
children.label: 2 Special rule for continuing violations
children.text: In the case of a continuing violation, the amount of the civil penalty may exceed the amount described in paragraph (1) but may not exceed the lesser of $1,000,000 per day or $5,000,000.

children.heading: Special rule for violations creating gain or loss
children.label: 3 Special rule for violations creating gain or loss
A
children.text: If any person derives pecuniary gain from the violation, or if the violation results in pecuniary loss to a person other than the violator, the amount of the civil penalty may exceed the amounts described in paragraphs (1) and (2) but may not exceed the amount of such gain or loss.

children.label: B
children.text: As used in this paragraph, the term “person” includes the Bank Insurance Fund, the Savings Association Insurance Fund, and after the merger of such funds, the Deposit Insurance Fund, and the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund.

children.heading: Violations to which penalty is applicable
children.label: c Violations to which penalty is applicable

children.text: section 287, 1001, 1032, 1341 or 1343 of title 18 affecting a federally insured financial institution; or1See 1990 Amendment note below.1

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: d Effective date
children.text: This section shall apply to violations occurring on or after .August 10, 1984

children.heading: Attorney General to bring action
children.label: e Attorney General to bring action
children.text: A civil action to recover a civil penalty under this section shall be commenced by the Attorney General.

children.heading: Burden of proof
children.label: f Burden of proof
children.text: In a civil action to recover a civil penalty under this section, the Attorney General must establish the right to recovery by a preponderance of the evidence.

children.label: C
children.text: by subpoena, summon witnesses and require the production of any books, papers, correspondence, memoranda, or other records which the Attorney General deems relevant or material to the inquiry. Such subpoena may require the attendance of witnesses and the production of any such records from any place in the United States at any place in the United States designated by the Attorney General.

children.heading: Procedures applicable
children.label: 2 Procedures applicable
children.text: The same procedures and limitations as are provided with respect to civil investigative demands in subsections (g), (h), and (j) of apply with respect to a subpoena issued under this subsection. Process required by such subsections to be served upon the custodian shall be served on the Attorney General. Failure to comply with an order of the court to enforce such subpoena shall be punishable as contempt.section 1968 of title 18

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: In the case of a subpoena for which the return date is less than 5 days after the date of service, no person shall be found in contempt for failure to comply by the return date if such person files a petition under paragraph (2) not later than 5 days after the date of service.

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: h Statute of limitations
children.text: A civil action under this section may not be commenced later than 10 years after the cause of action accrues.
